acceptance_targets: [ch06_booker_01] Pass if the revised opening establishes the family-infrastructure gap with one primary comparison cluster before the aid-office scene, and the later beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:230 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:348 each introduce a new consequence rather than another functionally identical comparison. Fail if the same class-gap observation is still being re-stated in multiple campus tableaux.

[ch06_craft_01] Pass if the revised requirement list and aid-office explanation point to one aid year that fits the continuity sheet's early-July-2019 timeline, and every later mention of the transcript or W-2s in this chapter refers to that same year. Fail if the chapter still asks for 2018 parent tax records without changing the chapter's canonical date.

[FBR-004] Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2303 through snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2313 contain one clear withheld-speech beat and then move directly into dialogue/action without a second "almost said" / "instead said" variation. Fail if the chapter still stacks the same suppression trick more than once in the local span.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised opening establishes the family-infrastructure gap with one primary comparison cluster before the aid-office scene, and the later beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:230 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:348 each introduce a new consequence rather than another functionally identical comparison. Fail if the same class-gap observation is still being re-stated in multiple campus tableaux.
findings.problem: The chapter lands the class-gap beat immediately, then keeps restating it through new campus-family tableaux and summary statements. Because the core engine is supposed to move from orientation optimism into procedural jeopardy, these repeated 'other people had somebody' beats start to re-announce the same emotional fact instead of escalating the chapter.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or differentiate the early comparison material at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:3 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:39, then repurpose the later returns at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:230 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:348 so each one adds new leverage: pending-status consequences, what Tiana now knows about Delroy, or what Malik's witness role changes for her.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised requirement list and aid-office explanation point to one aid year that fits the continuity sheet's early-July-2019 timeline, and every later mention of the transcript or W-2s in this chapter refers to that same year. Fail if the chapter still asks for 2018 parent tax records without changing the chapter's canonical date.
findings.problem: The chapter's procedural hinge contradicts the story's early-July-2019 continuity. In that timeline, FAFSA verification for an incoming fall 2019 student would not be anchored to 2018 parent tax documents, so the red-list paperwork reads like the wrong aid cycle. Because the chapter's power depends on concrete bureaucratic specificity, this date-year mismatch damages both realism and continuity.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the requirement list and Ms. Herrera's explanation at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:97, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:98, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:99, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:104 so the requested tax year matches the canonical chapter timing, then keep Delroy/Tiana's later document references consistent with that same aid cycle in the surrounding exchange.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2303 through snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2313 contain one clear withheld-speech beat and then move directly into dialogue/action without a second "almost said" / "instead said" variation. Fail if the chapter still stacks the same suppression trick more than once in the local span.
findings.problem: A recurring suppression-template construction appears in 7 chapters: "almost said" followed by withheld or redirected speech. This is the first and strongest use, so this chapter should KEEP the pattern only as one of at most two survivors in the novel; otherwise it reads as a book-wide workshop reflex rather than Tiana's specific self-control.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2307, but tighten snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2303 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2313 so the beat stays singular and is not restated into explanation. Do not add another withheld-speech construction elsewhere in this chapter. Let this one line stand as the chapter's single surviving instance of the full-book pattern.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into embarrassment, erotic consequence, moral compromise, and the ugly arithmetic of poverty. When a scene turns on prejudice, bureaucracy, violence, or family betrayal, stay with the room long enough for the reader to feel what each choice costs. Resolve edge cases by choosing the more specific and less flattering truth unless it breaks established character logic.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, racism, injury, debt, hunger, or shame is rendered abstractly, rewrite until the bodily detail, social pressure, and changed leverage are visible on the page.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high — contractions are the spoken norm across the novel; uncontracted forms should appear only for emphasis, ceremony, legal language, or character-specific pressure.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Vary scene count, dialogue density, and chapter motor across the book: pair public humiliation with private aftermath, follow argument chapters with information-turn or weather chapters, preserve at least three genuinely quiet domestic or communal stretches, and never let more than three chapters in a row hinge on debate followed by decisive action.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary and concrete, with Caribbean-American texture carried naturally in syntax and reference rather than in italicized glossary behavior; lyrical when earned, blunt when the room turns brutal.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person that rotates by chapter; intimate enough for bodily shame and private contradiction, but alert to class performance, racial reading, and room dynamics.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate lean pressure sentences with occasional longer image-bearing sentences; lyric lift should arrive at emotional turns, never as constant prestige haze.
